evals.name: s1-route-init-keyword
evals.prompt:
You are testing /forge's routing. Read the SKILL.md at the skill path and apply its Phase 0 dispatch rules + Phase 0a (mode detection) + Phase 0b (recording label detection) to determine what would happen if a user typed:

  /forge init

Output a single JSON object on stdout describing the routing decision, then stop. Use null for fields that don't apply.

{
  "route": "init" | "export" | "run" | "task",
  "mode": "drive" | "spec" | null,
  "record_as": "<label string>" | null,
  "references_to_load": ["<filename>", ...]
}
evals.expected_output: JSON: { route: 'init', mode: null, record_as: null, references_to_load: ['init.md'] }
expectations: JSON has mode equal to null
JSON has record_as equal to null
JSON does NOT have references_to_load containing 'team-task.md', 'export.md', or 'run.md'

evals.name: s1-route-init-natural-install
evals.prompt:
You are testing /forge's routing. Read the SKILL.md at the skill path and apply its Phase 0 dispatch rules + Phase 0a + Phase 0b to determine what would happen if a user typed:

  /forge install forge here

Output a single JSON object on stdout describing the routing decision, then stop. Use null for fields that don't apply.

{
  "route": "init" | "export" | "run" | "task",
  "mode": "drive" | "spec" | null,
  "record_as": "<label string>" | null,
  "references_to_load": ["<filename>", ...]
}
evals.expected_output: PENDING. Currently fails — Phase 0 only matches init by first-word keyword. The desired behavior is for natural-language phrasings like 'install forge here' to also route to init. Will pass once Phase 0 is expanded.
